title: 💻 Mastering the Vibe: Claude Code Best Practices That Actually Work
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

*How I went from fumbling with AI-generated spaghetti code to building production-ready apps with Claude Code*. Three months ago, I was that developer who would throw a vague prompt at Claude Code, cross my fingers, and hope for the best. If you’re ready to move beyond the “ask and pray” approach to something that actually works consistently, here’s what I’ve learned about making Claude Code genuinely useful. Think of it as Claude’s memory bank for your project. This isn’t just configuration — it’s how you teach Claude to understand your specific codebase, conventions, and quirks. The magic isn’t in the format — there’s no required format for CLAUDE.md files — it’s in being specific about what matters for *your* project. But the real power comes from asking Claude to make a plan before coding and explicitly telling it not to code until you’ve confirmed that its plan looks good.

title: 💻 What Is Claude Code /simplify and /batch? The Built-In Commands That Fix Overengineering | MindStudio
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

# What Is Claude Code /simplify and /batch? Claude Code's /simplify and /batch commands help you clean up bloated code and run large refactors in parallel. Learn how to use them. Claude Code tackles this with two commands worth knowing: `/simplify` and `/batch`. The `/batch` command lets you run operations across multiple files simultaneously, so large refactors don’t require running the same prompt over and over. Here’s how both work and when to use them. ## What Claude Code Is. Claude Code is Anthropic’s terminal-based AI coding assistant. It connects to your codebase through the file system, which means it can see the actual state of your project rather than relying on what you paste into a chat window. Instead of writing a long prompt explaining what you want, you type a command and Claude handles the task with the right behavior built in. When you run `/simplify` on a file or function, Claude Code evaluates:.

title: 💻 Claude Code Batch Processing Complete Guide: /batch Command and Headless Mode in Practice - SmartScope
content:

* /batch Command: Parallel Execution of Large-Scale Code Changes. * Headless Mode (claude -p): CI/CD and Script Integration. # Claude Code Batch Processing Complete Guide: /batch Command and Headless Mode in Practice¶. The `/batch` command in Claude Code shines when you need to apply the same transformation across hundreds of files. * The `/batch` command executes codebase-wide migrations safely using **parallel agents + Git Worktree** isolation. * Headless mode (`claude -p`) provides **non-interactive integration** for CI/CD pipelines and shell scripts. Intermediate to advanced Claude Code users looking to scale up with large-scale changes and CI/CD integration. The latter is a non-interactive execution mode for embedding AI processing into CI/CD pipelines. | Aspect | /batch Command | Headless Mode (claude -p) |. Choose headless mode when embedding AI processing into existing CI/CD pipelines. `/batch` is a slash command executed within an interactive Claude Code session1. ## Headless Mode (claude -p): CI/CD and Script Integration¶.

Comprehensive canine allergy support skill for dogs with environmental allergies (especially dust mite allergy), interdigital cysts and furunculosis, and chronic atopic dermatitis. Use this skill whenever the user mentions their dog's allergies, itching, licking or chewing paws, interdigital cysts, hot spots, chronic skin infections, dust mites, environmental allergen control, or any of the common canine allergy medications (Apoquel, Zenrelia, Cytopoint, Atopica, Befrena, allergen-specific immunotherapy). Browse the MCP Market to get started. The Batch Processing skill for Claude Code empowers developers to handle high-volume tasks—such as reviewing dozens of files or processing hundreds of documents—without linear increases in time or token costs. It provides a strategic framework for selecting the optimal execution method based on scale: utilizing agent swarms for rapid parallel processing of 5-20 items, the Anthropic API Batch for 50% cost savings on massive datasets, or sequential loops for tasks with complex dependencies.

So here is a small MCP that lets you send work directly to Anthropic's Batch API from inside Claude Code, for the same quality responses just 50% cheaper, results come back in ~30min-1hr. ```  How it works: you type /batch review this codebase for security issues, Claude gathers all the context, builds a self-contained prompt, ships it to the Batch API via an MCP server, and you get notified in the status bar when it's done (optional).

The toolkit reads `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` from `~/.claude/env`. #### Step 6: Register the MCP server in `~/.claude.json`. #### Step 7 *(optional)*: Configure the statusline in `~/.claude/settings.json`.
